title: Industrialization Pathways Comparison
description: Compare how Britain, Japan, and Qing China experienced industrial-era transformation under different state, market, and geopolitical conditions.
learning_goals: Compare how market-led and state-led modernization produced different trajectories.
Identify shared industrial drivers such as technology adoption and military pressure.
Practice explaining why outcomes diverged across Britain, Japan, and Qing China.

comparison_prompts.shared_driver: Both transformed institutions to support industrial expansion.
comparison_prompts.contrast_note: Britain's factory system scaled through private capital and commercial networks, while Meiji Japan used central-state reform to remove regional barriers and coordinate modernization.

comparison_prompts.shared_driver: Industrial-era trade pressures reshaped state priorities.
comparison_prompts.contrast_note: Britain leveraged transport innovation to deepen industrial markets; Qing China faced coercive treaty-port integration after military defeat, constraining sovereign control over modernization timing.

comparison_prompts.shared_driver: Both linked modern industry with military and state capacity.
comparison_prompts.contrast_note: In Japan, military conscription and state modernization advanced together; in Britain and wider Europe, late-century industrial innovation diffused through established industrial ecosystems and private firms.
